title: Stellar Coronagraphy PSF Subtraction
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Stellar Coronagraphy PSF Subtraction: High-contrast imaging: subtract stellar PSF residuals (speckles) to detect faint companions using reference differential imaging or ADI. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (K.psf.coronagraph…); recovery is posed as a linear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; speckle_evolution_rate, wavefront_error_drift set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: companion flux map f(r,theta) in R^{H x W}, contrast ~1e-5 to 1e-8
E.observation_y: focal plane intensity image I in R^{H x W} (speckle + shot noise dominated)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D_companion_flux is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); speckle_evolution_rate dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Shot poisson sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: 5-sigma contrast floor scales as sqrt(N_frames) with speckle noise. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |speckle_evolution_rate|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_frames * H * W * K) for K-component KLIP per iteration
